required_preferences.fact: Strictly avoids foods with the suspected allergen; researches and eats allergy-safe international cuisine at home and out, avoiding both Mediterranean staples and American fast food

supersedes.fact: Sticks strictly to the Mediterranean diet while in France — avoids American fast food or snacks

required_preferences.fact: No longer uses a sleep mask or earplugs at night; instead, implements new physician-recommended sleep hygiene strategies adapted for a noisy dorm environment (e.g., set bedtime routine, calming tea, lights-out protocol).

supersedes.fact: Uses a sleep mask and earplugs every night in the dorm — never attempts to sleep without them

required_preferences.fact: Drinks herbal or calming tea and follows a fixed bedtime routine (reading, dim light, phone put away) for at least 30 minutes before attempting sleep each night in the dorm.
Carefully reviews ingredient lists and allergen warnings when shopping at neighborhood markets; never purchases unpackaged foods without clear labels

Prefers to meet French group project partners at cozy, off-campus cafés in the city for collaborative planning sessions — avoids studying in the dorm due to distractions and language-switching with international roommates
Shifts sleep routine to later nights while her sister visits, allowing for extended evening conversations despite her usual early bedtime

persona: An exchange student living abroad for a semester
